description: Adversarial invariants from the manual accuracy sample: keep early electrical motors and grids from depending on later generation technologies, while preserving the distinct 1831 Faraday-generator and 1866 industrial-dynamo milestones.

checks.reason: Faraday's 1821 electric motor predates the graph's 1866 self-excited industrial dynamo.
Faraday's 1821 electric motor predates his distinct 1831 disk-generator experiment.
Early 1882 central distribution systems predate Parsons's 1884 steam turbine.
The electric motor node is scoped to Faraday's first electromagnetic-rotation motor.
The Faraday Disk Generator node preserves the 1831 experimental generator milestone after the 1821 motor principle.
The Dynamo node is scoped separately to the 1866 self-excited industrial machine.
The grid node is scoped to early central distribution such as Pearl Street.
The steam-turbine node should remain later than early central electrical distribution.
